Six products,

case_1.quote: An international trading site for VDEMA LTD — covering commodity listings, warehouse capacity, and buyer enquiries across the US, Europe and Africa.

case_2.quote: Jemo.cm — our in-house e-commerce platform positioned as Cameroon's trusted shop, built around Mobile Money checkout and local delivery from day one.

case_3.quote: A full lounge management system for Beverly's Lounge in Bonamoussadi — orders, stock and staff rotations unified where there used to be three notebooks.

process.subtitle: One team from the first call to the quarterly review — no agency-to-freelancer shuffle.

step_1.title: Discover
step_1.description: Workshop your goals, users and constraints. Audit what you already run.

step_2.title: Design
step_2.description: High-fidelity prototypes you can click before a line of code is written.

step_3.title: Build
step_3.description: Ship in fortnightly slices. Real product you can use — not milestones on a chart.

step_4.title: Grow
step_4.description: Iterate on real usage. Monthly metrics review. We stick around past launch.

testimonial_1.quote: BridgeYou delivered a site that speaks to international buyers — clean, professional, and built to handle commodity trading across three continents. They understood our scale.
testimonial_1.author: Van Donald Antiero

testimonial_2.quote: They built Primeselar from the ground up. The platform now handles our entire digital services catalog — subscriptions, streaming, virtual numbers — all paid with MTN and Orange Money.
testimonial_2.author: Nkenfack Teddy

testimonial_3.quote: Our orders, stock and staff shifts used to live in three different notebooks. BridgeYou put everything in one system — we run the lounge with two fewer headaches every night.
testimonial_3.author: Beverly's Lounge team

cta_banner.subtitle: 30-minute call, no obligation. We'll walk through your goals, your stack, and what a pilot could look like.

q1.question: How long until we go live?
q1.answer: Most websites ship in 3–4 weeks. Booking systems and CRMs take 6–10 weeks depending on integrations. We break the work into fortnightly slices you can review and sign off.

q2.question: Do you accept Mobile Money payments?
q2.answer: Yes. MTN MoMo, Orange Money and card payments all work out of the box. We also integrate with your bank if you need direct settlement.

q3.question: What happens after launch?
q3.answer: Every project includes 30 days of support and a handover doc. After that, you can stay on a monthly growth retainer or pay-as-you-go for updates.

q4.question: Can you work in both English and French?
q4.answer: Yes — all of our sites, systems and support are fully bilingual. We write copy in both languages from day one.

q5.question: Do you build for industries outside Cameroon?
q5.answer: We work with teams across Africa and globally. Our base is in Cameroon but the stack, process and Mobile Money integrations transfer across the continent.

q6.question: How do you price projects?
q6.answer: Fixed-price for scoped work, monthly retainer for ongoing growth. We walk you through a clear estimate after the first call — no surprises later.

company.page_heading_rich:
Build the software layer of
modern African business.
company.page_subtitle: We're a Douala-based studio serving clients across Cameroon and beyond — building the tools we wish existed, tuned for how businesses on the continent actually operate.
company.registered_intro: BridgeYou Innovations SARL is officially registered in Cameroon. We issue proper invoices, operate under a formal tax regime, and our incorporation documents are available on request.
company.mission_text: We build software the way we'd want it built for us — simple, honest, and measured by real outcomes. We're partners, not vendors. And we're still here after launch.

value_1.title: Simplicity
value_1.description: Easy to use, not just powerful. Every click earns its place.

value_2.title: Results
value_2.description: We measure success by the impact we create for your business.

value_3.title: Partnership
value_3.description: We're not vendors — we're partners invested in your growth.

value_4.title: Craft
value_4.description: We care how it looks, feels, and performs — to the last pixel.

founder.name: Nfon Joseph Azenui
founder.bio: I founded BridgeYou to build the software African businesses actually need — websites, booking systems, CRMs and automations shaped around how teams here operate. From Douala, I partner directly with each client from the first call to launch and beyond.
